sources.notes: Used to confirm the subsequence-balance coefficient formula and the four-state amortized structure for tracking middle deletions.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Maintain a dynamically transformed sequence and after each operation output the sum of balance values over all nonempty subsequences.
annotation.constraints: The number of operations is large and type 2 can double the sequence length, so neither the sequence nor all subsequences can be represented explicitly.
annotation.core_idea: First prove a global combinatorial identity: over all subsequences of a fixed length, symmetry of a subsequence and its reversal makes every array element have the same coefficient, so the total balance of all subsequences depends only on current length n and current sum S. The coefficient can be precomputed/updated with binomial identities. Then maintain only n, S, and the value removed by each middle-deletion operation. Since type 2 inserts the same value into every gap, the middle value can be tracked through a small four-state recursive/stack structure; each stored block is advanced or removed amortized O(1) times.
annotation.complexity: O(q log MOD) or O(q) with precomputed inverses, depending on coefficient implementation.
tricks: answer depends only on length and sum

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Use the combinatorial coefficient to answer from (n, sum), while an amortized four-state structure supplies the middle element removed by type 1 operations.
solution_variants.complexity: O(q log MOD) or O(q) after precomputation

tags.evidence: The answer formula sums coefficients over all subsequence lengths and simplifies with binomial identities.
tags.description: Use algebraic identities involving binomial coefficients to collapse a sum, recurrence, or counting expression.
tags.created_reason: This is more specific than generic combinatorics and useful for problems whose main step is simplifying binomial sums.

tags.evidence: Pairing subsequences with their reversals makes each original element contribute equally to the global balance sum.
tags.description: Exploit symmetry, reversal, complement, or paired configurations to equalize contributions or simplify counting.
tags.created_reason: The decisive proof is a symmetry argument not captured by broad math/combinatorics tags.

tags.evidence: The implicit middle tracker advances through stored blocks so each block/state is removed or updated only a constant number of times.
The huge sequence is represented by a small recursive state instead of explicit positions.
The output is a sum over all subsequences collapsed through counting coefficients.
The hard part after the formula is carefully maintaining the implicit sequence state through updates.
